pattern: The tree's height fixes the grid size, and placing each node at a column that halves the horizontal offset as depth increases lays the structure out symmetrically in a string matrix.
hints: Before placing anything, you need the grid dimensions; the row count is the height plus one and the column count grows like a full tree of that height.
The root goes in the exact middle of the top row, and each child sits halfway between its parent's column and the edge of its half of the grid.
Recurse with the current row, the parent's column, and a shrinking offset; the left child uses column minus offset, the right child column plus offset, and the offset halves each level down.

edge_inputs.description: single node fills a one-cell grid
negative values still occupy one cell each

stress.description: random tree of bounded depth serialized level-order
stress.note: bounded depth keeps the output matrix from blowing up
